island_map.lighthouse_note: Visible on the horizon from Ch1 onward. Never locked from view, always unreachable until Ch6. Serves as narrative north star.

areas.theme: A once-bustling fishing harbor now silent and salt-worn. Wooden docks are warped, nets tangled in rusted moorings, the fish-market stalls closed with peeling paint. The smell of brine still lingers — the sea never left, only the people did.
areas.full_restoration_visual: At dusk, the restored fishing lanterns ignite one by one across the harbor. Warm amber-orange light reflects in the water in long rippling columns. The silhouettes of repaired boats sway gently. An NPC fisherman hangs a lantern from his bow. The harbor hums with the low murmur of evening tide.
areas.hinata_unlock_line_jp: 次は山の向こうに工房があるんです。おじいちゃんがよく、あそこの土は魂を持っていると言っていました。

visual_states_detail.desolate: Greyscale palette. Splintered wooden docks, overturned boats half-submerged. Nets drag in still water. A broken lantern pole stands askew. The fish-market awning is torn. No NPCs. Low-contrast fog at the water edge.
visual_states_detail.stirring: A single lantern has been re-hung and flickers weakly. One boat is upright and cleaned. Driftwood has been cleared from the dock boards. A pile of gathered materials is visible near the market stall. Colour begins to return — muted sea-blue, weathered gold.
visual_states_detail.blooming: Three boats are restored and moored neatly. The fish-market stalls are open with cloth awnings in dusty red and cream. Two NPC fishermen move along the dock. Seagulls circle overhead. The water shimmers with hints of teal and green.
visual_states_detail.radiant: Full evening scene. Fishing lanterns illuminate the entire harbor in warm amber. Water surface reflects lantern light in dancing columns. The market stall serves grilled fish. The old fisherman NPC sits on the dock edge watching the light on the water. Stars begin to emerge above.

restoration_stages.narrative_beat: Ritsu clears the dock of driftwood and re-hangs the first lantern.
The fish market reopens. Hinata brings a pot of warm tea to the dock.
All lanterns lit at dusk. The harbor feels alive again for the first time in years.

areas.theme: A hilltop pottery studio behind the harbor, once famous across the island for its glazed work. The kiln has been cold for years. Clay-dust coats every surface. Half-finished pieces sit on shelves, abandoned mid-form — time frozen at the moment someone stopped caring.
areas.full_restoration_visual: The kiln fires up for the first time in years. The door glows a deep, pulsing orange from within. Heat shimmer rises from the chimney. Two NPCs, a potter and an apprentice, watch with quiet reverence as the kiln temperature climbs. The warm orange light spills across the courtyard against the evening sky.
areas.hinata_unlock_line_jp: 海辺の森、覚えてます？子どもの頃よく走り回っていたんです。今はどうなっているのか、怖くて見に行けなくて。

visual_states_detail.desolate: Muted terracotta and ash grey. The kiln is cold and cracked at its base. Pottery shards litter the floor. Shelves carry dusty half-thrown forms. The wooden door hangs on one hinge. A potter's wheel sits still, clay dried hard on its surface.
visual_states_detail.stirring: The floor has been swept. The potter's wheel is cleaned and turning slowly. Fresh clay has been brought in — a mound sits ready beside the wheel. The kiln's exterior has been patched and soot removed. Warm earthy tones begin to emerge.
visual_states_detail.blooming: Three shelves bear completed, glazed pottery in soft blue and cream. An NPC potter works at the wheel, focused and slow. A second NPC stacks pieces for firing. The courtyard has a display table with finished pieces. Warm terracotta and leaf-green palette.
visual_states_detail.radiant: The kiln glows orange, door ajar, heat-light playing across the entire studio. Finished glazed pieces line the windowsill. The potter pauses their work and bows slightly toward the kiln, a private moment. The scent of warm clay and wood-smoke is implicit in every visual cue.

restoration_stages.narrative_beat: Ritsu clears the studio and finds 渉's old glazing notes pinned to the wall.
The first new pots are finished. Hinata picks up a small one and turns it in her hands.
The kiln fires for the first time in years. The island smells different tonight.

areas.theme: A narrow forest between the hillside and the sea cliffs, once threaded with paths that children ran through in summer. The undergrowth is choked and overgrown, the path signs weathered to illegibility. But the trees are still tall — they only needed someone to clear the way beneath them.
areas.full_restoration_visual: Cherry blossom trees the player cleared and planted along the restored path erupt in bloom simultaneously. The path becomes a tunnel of white and pale pink petals. A single petal drifts down and lands on Hinata's hair. She doesn't notice — she's looking ahead at the light through the trees.
areas.hinata_unlock_line_jp: 石畳の小道、もうずっと誰も歩いていないと思う。でも、きっとまだそこにあるはずだから。

visual_states_detail.desolate: Overgrown in dark blue-green shadow. The path is invisible beneath dense undergrowth. A rusted path-sign points nowhere. Fallen branches block progress. The light barely penetrates the canopy. Moss covers everything indiscriminately — not the good kind of moss.
visual_states_detail.stirring: A narrow path has been cleared — just wide enough to walk single-file. New moss has been placed deliberately at path edges, soft and bright green. A single seedling in a cleared patch reaches toward filtered light. The forest feels breathable again.
visual_states_detail.blooming: The path is wide and clearly defined, bordered by young ferns and placed stones. Two NPC children run through — a deliberate echo of 渉's memory. Dappled light plays through fresh leaf growth. The palette is soft green, cream, and gold.
visual_states_detail.radiant: Cherry blossom trees in full bloom create a canopy arch over the path. Petals drift continuously. The path glows with filtered pink-white light. At the far end of the path, barely visible, the cliff begins — the next chapter foreshadowed in the beauty.

restoration_stages.narrative_beat: Ritsu cuts through the first overgrown section. Finds an old path-marker stone half-buried.
The full path is walkable. Hinata walks it quietly beside Ritsu without speaking.
The cherry blossoms all open at once. Hinata stops mid-path and looks up for a long moment.

areas.theme: The old paved road that once connected the forest to the cliffs — a civic project from the island's prosperous years. Many stones have sunk, cracked, or been displaced. Weeds grow in the gaps. But the stones themselves are still there, just waiting to be placed back where they belong.
areas.full_restoration_visual: At twilight, the newly restored stone lanterns along the path flicker on in sequence — first one, then the next, then the next — until the whole path glows in a warm amber chain stretching from forest to cliff. The effect is of a landing strip for stars.
areas.hinata_unlock_line_jp: 崖に出ると、空が全部見えるんです。晴れた夜は、星が落ちてくるみたいで。

visual_states_detail.desolate: Cracked and sunken paving stones, half-buried in soil. The stone lanterns are toppled or dark, their glass cracked. Weeds push through every joint. The path's edges have eroded into the surrounding grass. Cold grey-blue palette.
visual_states_detail.stirring: Stones have been levelled in the first section. A single lantern has been re-erected and its glass replaced, unlit but intact. The path edges are trimmed. The stonework's beauty — its careful hand-laying — begins to show through again.
visual_states_detail.blooming: Most of the path is restored and level. Stone lanterns stand upright with new glass at regular intervals. An NPC elder walks the path slowly, tapping a cane, clearly pleased. Moss is growing in the joints deliberately — the soft, chosen kind.
visual_states_detail.radiant: All lanterns alight at twilight in sequence. The amber chain of light stretches the full path length. The stones glow warm underfoot. The path feels ancient and cared-for simultaneously — like it was always meant to exist exactly this way.

restoration_stages.narrative_beat: Ritsu levels the first section of path. Finds a stone with a name carved in it — 丸山.
The path connects fully. Hinata traces the carved name on the stone with one finger.
The lanterns light at dusk. Ritsu and Hinata stand at the path's end together without words.

areas.theme: A stone viewing platform at the island's highest point, weathered but structurally sound. The railing has fallen away on one side. The engraved information plaques are unreadable. But the view — the unbounded sea and open sky — has never once changed. It is the most honest place on the island.
areas.full_restoration_visual: At chapter end, a time-lapse sequence plays: the sky darkens over the restored lookout, clouds dissipate, and stars emerge one by one until the full night sky is blazing overhead. The Milky Way arcs above the cliff. In the corner of the frame, the distant lighthouse beam sweeps past once — a promise of what comes next.
areas.hinata_unlock_line_jp: 灯台には、おじいちゃんの全てが詰まってる気がして。ひとりでは、まだ上がれなかったんです。

visual_states_detail.desolate: Crumbling stone railing, missing sections. The wooden viewing bench is rotted. The information plaques are blank with weathering. Overgrown with wind-resistant scrub. The sky above is perpetually overcast in this state — the view is there but grey and obscured.
visual_states_detail.stirring: The dangerous railing section has been temporarily blocked with rope. The bench is cleared and structurally sound. Quartz fragments have been gathered and placed at the platform edge, catching what light exists. The sky begins to show through cloud gaps.
visual_states_detail.blooming: A new stone railing is in place, fitted with quartz inlays that catch sunlight. The bench is restored. An NPC couple sits at the viewpoint looking at the horizon. The sky is clear, the sea blue. The view shows, in the far distance, the lighthouse on its rock.
visual_states_detail.radiant: Night-time star-field time-lapse. The repaired quartz railing catches starlight and seems to glow faintly from within. The platform is silent except for wind. The lighthouse beam sweeps across the frame from the distance — first appearance of the active lighthouse visual.

restoration_stages.narrative_beat: Ritsu makes the lookout safe. Hinata comes for the first time in years, stops at the railing.
The new railing is set with quartz. Hinata says she used to come here with her grandfather.
Stars emerge in time-lapse. The distant lighthouse beam sweeps across for the first time.

areas.theme: The lighthouse at the island's seaward edge — visible from everywhere, reached by none until now. The lens is intact but fogged and cracked in places. The mechanism that rotates the beam is seized with rust. The keeper's cottage at its base has been empty since 渉 died. Everything here is a form of grief waiting to be resolved.
areas.full_restoration_visual: The lighthouse beam activates for the first time in years and sweeps slowly across the dark sea. The beam is warm gold, not harsh white. Ships far out respond — tiny lights blinking acknowledgement. Inside the lantern room, the rotating lens mechanism turns smooth and sure. Hinata is in the room when it starts, and her face in the rotating light is the emotional climax of the game.

visual_states_detail.desolate: The lighthouse tower stands intact but dark, its lens chamber black. The keeper's cottage windows are boarded. The path up to the lighthouse door is overgrown. Oil stains mark where the lamp last burned, decades cold. The lighthouse is the only structure in the game that feels haunted rather than merely abandoned.
visual_states_detail.stirring: The path to the lighthouse door has been cleared. The cottage boards have been removed from one window — warm empty interior visible. The lens chamber has been accessed; the cracked glass panels are stacked, awaiting replacement. Oil lanterns around the base have been lit — small lights around the dark tower.
visual_states_detail.blooming: The cottage is inhabited — Hinata has moved back in. Light in the cottage windows at night. The exterior path is fully restored. The lens chamber glass has been replaced; it catches light during the day and scatters prismatic color. The beam is not yet active — the mechanism is being repaired.
visual_states_detail.radiant: The beam activates. Warm gold light sweeps across sea and island. Every previously restored area catches the beam for one second each as it passes — the harbor lanterns, the kiln glow, the cherry blossoms, the stone path lanterns, the cliff quartz — all briefly illuminated by the lighthouse. The island is whole.

restoration_stages.narrative_beat: The path to the lighthouse is cleared. Ritsu reads 渉's last journal entry inside the cottage.
Hinata moves back into the cottage. She and Ritsu work on the lens mechanism together.
The beam sweeps across the sea. Hinata watches from the lantern room. The island is whole.

chapter_unlock_ceremony.description: Six-step transition sequence triggered when a chapter's final level is completed.

steps.detail: Level complete screen fades. Star count displayed briefly then fades to black.

Chapter N area transitions to its radiant state with a full-area glow animation. Signature visual plays: harbor lanterns / kiln fire / cherry blossoms / stone lanterns / star time-lapse / lighthouse beam.
Camera pulls back to island map, then pans to the adjacent locked area. Fog lifts in a soft 2-second dissolve to reveal the area silhouette in muted tones. Soft swell music rises.
Hinata's character portrait appears at lower third. She delivers a single spoken line (Japanese) with subtitle. Line teases the next chapter's theme without spoiling the story beat.

lines_per_chapter_completed.ch1_to_ch2: 次は山の向こうに工房があるんです。おじいちゃんがよく、あそこの土は魂を持っていると言っていました。
lines_per_chapter_completed.ch2_to_ch3: 海辺の森、覚えてます？子どもの頃よく走り回っていたんです。今はどうなっているのか、怖くて見に行けなくて。
lines_per_chapter_completed.ch3_to_ch4: 石畳の小道、もうずっと誰も歩いていないと思う。でも、きっとまだそこにあるはずだから。
lines_per_chapter_completed.ch4_to_ch5: 崖に出ると、空が全部見えるんです。晴れた夜は、星が落ちてくるみたいで。
lines_per_chapter_completed.ch5_to_ch6: 灯台には、おじいちゃんの全てが詰まってる気がして。ひとりでは、まだ上がれなかったんです。

steps.detail: New chapter area glows gold briefly. Three level nodes appear with a soft unlocking chime. First 3 levels of the new chapter become available.

A journal page floats up from the restored area with a paper-rustle sound. '渉の記憶 — 第N章' appears briefly. The page folds into the Memory Room icon. A soft notification confirms: 'Memory Room に記録が追加されました'.

memory_fragment_system.all_collected_reward_detail: A full-page illustrated scene of 渉 and young ひなた at the lighthouse, warm evening light, drawn in the game's pastoral illustration style. Viewable in the Memory Room as a seventh 'page'. No gameplay reward — pure narrative closure.

presentation.typography: brushstroke-style Japanese font, slightly uneven baseline to suggest handwriting
presentation.paper_texture: aged cream with soft edge vignette, faint horizontal ruled lines

memory_fragment_system.access: Memory Room accessible from island map at any time. Locked fragments shown as sealed envelopes.

fragments.theme: Harbor mornings — the smell of the sea, the fishermen's rhythm
fragments.prose_jp: 夜明け前の漁港が好きだった。まだ暗いうちから灯籠の明かりが水に揺れて、漁師たちが言葉少なに網を整える。あの静けさの中に、島の心拍があった。海の匂いと、人が働く音と。それだけで十分だった。
fragments.visual_prompt: An aged journal page. Harbor at pre-dawn, ink-wash style. A single lantern reflected in dark water. A fisherman's silhouette at dock edge.

fragments.theme: The patience of craft — a potter's lesson applied to life
fragments.prose_jp: 土は急かされるのを嫌う。焦れば割れる。ただ手のひらで包んで、回しながら待てばいい。人も同じかもしれない。壊れかけた心を直す方法も、きっとそれに近い——急がずに、ただそばにいることだ。
fragments.visual_prompt: A potter's wheel in ink wash. Hands cupping wet clay, unhurried. Kiln light visible in background doorway.

fragments.theme: Young Hinata running through the forest — she didn't know he was watching
fragments.prose_jp: ひなたは気づいていなかっただろう、私が木の陰から見ていたことを。あの子は夏の森を風みたいに走っていた。笑い声が枝の間に消えて、また戻ってくる。ただそれを見ていた。あれが幸せというものだった。
fragments.visual_prompt: Ink-wash forest path. A child's figure in motion blur between trees, small and bright against the deep greens. An older figure's shadow in the foreground, still, watching.

fragments.theme: A solitary walk when the island was empty — what he heard in the silence
fragments.prose_jp: 人が去った後も、石畳は残っていた。ひとりで歩くと、足音がよく響いた。静かな島は寂しくもあったが、正直でもあった。風の音、草の揺れ、遠い波。それらは誰かのためでなく、ただそこにあり続けていた。
fragments.visual_prompt: Empty stone path in ink-wash. No figures. Stone lanterns dark. Wind-bent grass at the edges. The path continues into the frame's distance.

fragments.theme: Stars from the cliff — smallness as relief, not loneliness
fragments.prose_jp: 星空の下に立つと、自分がどれほど小さいか分かる。若い頃はそれが怖かった。でも年を取るにつれて、あの小ささが楽になってきた。何も抱えなくていい、という感覚。ただの点として、宇宙の中にいる安心感。
fragments.visual_prompt: Ink-wash cliff edge at night. A single figure, very small, under an enormous star field. The Milky Way rendered in fine spatter. The figure faces the sky, not the sea.

fragments.theme: The letter to Ritsu — why he chose this particular stranger, the hope
fragments.prose_jp: あなたのことを新聞で読んだとき、理由は分からないが確信した。疲れた人間にしか、壊れた場所の美しさは見えない。島はあなたを必要としている。そして、もしかしたらあなたも、島を必要としているのかもしれないと思った。
fragments.visual_prompt: The letter itself, ink-wash. 渉's handwriting, careful and old. A lighthouse drawn small in the margin. The salutation visible: '山田律様へ'. The final line trails off, unfinished.

drop_mechanics.normal_drop_quantity_note: Randomised 2-5 units per level completion. Higher chapter levels yield toward the upper range.
drop_mechanics.rare_drop_note: A 4+ combo chain during a level generates a 15% chance of a rare material bonus drop at level end. Visually: a glowing material icon pulses during the chain.

material_system.soft_cap_behaviour: At 99, material icons display a soft gold border. Overflow during active play is permitted — materials collected mid-level are granted in full even if they briefly exceed 99. No hard block.
material_system.material_display: Materials shown in a panel on the island map screen. Each type has a small illustrated icon matching its chapter aesthetic.

map_interaction_design.tap_restored_area: Shows area name, restoration percentage, and a visual breakdown of stages completed. A small animated vignette of the area's current visual state plays.
map_interaction_design.tap_locked_area: Shows fog-silhouette with area name visible but story text hidden. A 'locked' icon with the chapter number. Tap again shows: 'Complete [previous chapter] to reveal.'
map_interaction_design.tap_lighthouse_any_chapter: Ch1-5: Shows lighthouse silhouette on the horizon with a soft pulse. Hinata's voice line: 'いつかあそこまで、一緒に行けたらいいですね。' Ch6: Lighthouse becomes interactive.

fog_lift_animation.style: Fog dissolves from center outward. Area silhouette solidifies from grey to desaturated colour.
fog_lift_animation.music: Soft swell — single sustained note on a koto, rising.

map_interaction_design.lighthouse_on_horizon_note: Lighthouse is rendered in the island map background from Ch1. It is visually present but placed at the map's far edge — no tap target until Ch6 unlock. It functions as a constant horizon-line goal.

design_rationale.philosophy: Glow Island is designed as a finite, timeless experience — a complete arc with a beginning, middle, and end. Every system supports this rather than fighting against it.
design_rationale.why_timeless: Seasonal content and battle passes create FOMO — a low-grade anxiety that the player will miss something irreversible. This is antithetical to a game about a burned-out protagonist recovering. The meta-game must be the opposite of the conditions that caused the burnout: no deadlines, no expiring content, no competitive pressure. The island waits for the player. It will always wait.

create: Finite narrative arc as primary engagement driver
Memory fragment collection as completionist reward
No-pressure restoration loop that respects player time

design_rationale.engagement_source_detail: The primary engagement hook is 渉's story — a dead man who chose a stranger to inherit his love for an island. The desire to know why he chose Ritsu, and to read his final journal entry, is a stronger pull than any daily reward. The player earns emotional payoff, not currency.
design_rationale.fomo_replacement_detail: FOMO (fear of missing out on limited-time content) is replaced by JOMO (joy of missing out on pressure) and replaced with completionist satisfaction. The six memory fragments create a finite, fully achievable collection. All 6 collected unlocks a special illustration — not a gameplay advantage, just beauty and closure. Players who want to see 渉's full story have a clear, achievable path that will never expire.
design_rationale.finite_endpoint_design: The lighthouse beam activation is designed as the natural end of the restoration arc. After this moment, the island is whole. The game does not extend past this — there is no post-game grind. The 'end state' is a living, restored island that the player can revisit and walk through at leisure. The endpoint is not a locked door; it is an open, warm room.
design_rationale.long_term_replayability: Players who want to replay start fresh on a new island. The emotional experience of reading 渉's fragments for the first time is irreplaceable — but the second playthrough reveals small details in the fragments that were invisible the first time, creating a New Game+ quality without explicit mechanics.
design_rationale.no_battlepass_rationale_summary: A battle pass would require seasonal theming, which would require Kagayaki-jima to be re-decorated seasonally — destroying the sense of a real place with real history. The island should feel as though it was always there, not as though it's being dressed for a marketing event.
